jokes.text: Your lips look a little cracked.
jokes.setup: Post-show wind-down with casual conversation
jokes.punchline: Blunt observation about Larry's appearance
jokes.explanation: Simple industry observation that sets the tone - functional but not special

dialogue.line: Your lips look a little cracked.

jokes.text: Of course. They misplaced the lip balm.
jokes.setup: Response to lip crack observation
jokes.punchline: Self-important blame deflection
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's tendency to blame others for minor personal issues - good character work

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Of course. They misplaced the lip balm.

jokes.text: Excuse me. I hate to be a bother, but is there some religious reason you're not answering my phone?
jokes.setup: Hank's phone going unanswered
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive sarcasm with 'religious reason'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank line - petty, theatrical, passive-aggressive. Great character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Excuse me. I hate to be a bother, but is there some religious reason you're not answering my phone?

jokes.text: What darlene does is not a job, you see? It's a calling, and I will wait for her return.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to hire a temp
jokes.punchline: Hank's overwrought devotion to his assistant
jokes.explanation: Hank's melodramatic attachment to his assistant is perfectly over-the-top and character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: What darlene does is not a job, you see? It's a calling, and I will wait for her return.

jokes.text: Hey, look out, me boy, I found me lucky poker hat.
jokes.setup: Artie appears with something on his head
jokes.punchline: Irish accent and superstitious poker ritual
jokes.explanation: Standard Artie character comedy - gruff but endearing

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Hey, look out, me boy, I found me lucky poker hat.

jokes.text: Wayne's world money!
jokes.setup: Dana Carvey poker game discussion
jokes.punchline: Reference to Carvey's biggest commercial success
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that works for the era but isn't particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Wayne's world money!

jokes.text: Artie wearing the Dana Carvey tape as a hat
jokes.setup: Larry looking for the Dana Carvey tape
jokes.punchline: Artie unknowingly wearing it under his 'lucky hat'
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy gold - the setup and payoff are perfectly timed and visual

jokes.text: I think he's on crack.
jokes.setup: Continuation of Lorne Michaels comment
jokes.punchline: Escalation to drug accusation
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry escalation but fairly predictable

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I think he's on crack.

jokes.text: You're getting very wide. I'm shoving it up.
jokes.setup: Larry can't see TV, Artie blocking view
jokes.punchline: Artie's blunt response to being called wide
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment with authentic character interaction

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You're getting very wide.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I'm shoving it up.

jokes.text: Chinless little cocksuckers.
jokes.setup: Larry's insecurity about his chin in the impression
jokes.punchline: Artie's crude but supportive insult of SNL cast
jokes.explanation: Perfect Artie - crude but loyal, and the specificity of 'chinless' is great

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Chinless little cocksuckers.

jokes.text: Well, you... you lilt.
jokes.setup: Larry asking if he whines like the impression
jokes.punchline: Artie's diplomatic but accurate correction
jokes.explanation: Perfectly chosen word that's both diplomatic and brutally accurate

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Well, you... you lilt.

jokes.text: Fuck you.
jokes.setup: Hank answering his phone
jokes.punchline: Immediate hostile greeting
jokes.explanation: Shows Hank's frazzled state, economy of language works well

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Fuck you.

jokes.text: I'm tracing this call right now! Pound sand up your ass.
jokes.setup: Another phone call interruption
jokes.punchline: Hank's increasingly hostile phone etiquette
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from the previous phone moment, 'pound sand up your ass' is colorful

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I'm tracing this call right now! Pound sand up your ass.

jokes.text: From the cirque du soleil. The sad clown, right?
jokes.setup: Meeting Brian for the first time
jokes.punchline: Hank's confused assumption about Brian's appearance
jokes.explanation: Shows Hank's tendency to make odd assumptions, reasonably funny

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: From the cirque du soleil. The sad clown, right?

jokes.text: Remember? Everybody put on their protective goggles, and let's all play double danger. Double danger.
jokes.setup: Brian showing his fandom knowledge
jokes.punchline: Overly specific recall of Hank's old show details
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Hank's past, shows Brian's knowledge

dialogue.speaker: Brian
dialogue.line: Remember? Everybody put on their protective goggles, and let's all play double danger. Double danger.

jokes.text: Well, actually, I was the personal assistant to her personal assistant.
jokes.setup: Impressive resume item about Barbara Streisand
jokes.punchline: Deflating clarification of actual role
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of initial impression, realistic industry detail

dialogue.speaker: Brian
dialogue.line: Well, actually, I was the personal assistant to her personal assistant.

jokes.text: She made him clean the hair out of her tub.
jokes.setup: Brian can't say why he left due to confidentiality
jokes.punchline: Paula reveals the gross truth
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe detail that feels both gross and believable

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: She made him clean the hair out of her tub.

jokes.text: Oh, our nation's capital. Canada. I know! Canada.
jokes.setup: Brian says he's from British Columbia
jokes.punchline: Hank's geographical confusion
jokes.explanation: Shows Hank's lack of knowledge, moderately funny

dialogue.speaker: Brian
dialogue.line: Oh, our nation's capital.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Canada.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I know!

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Canada.

jokes.text: Don't speak to Larry unless he speaks to you.
jokes.setup: Artie giving Brian workplace advice
jokes.punchline: Revealing the toxic workplace dynamic
jokes.explanation: Perfect industry satire - reveals the hierarchy and Larry's ego

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Don't speak to Larry unless he speaks to you.

jokes.text: Fat lips.
jokes.setup: Larry asking about his appearance insecurities
jokes.punchline: Revealing his childhood nickname
jokes.explanation: Good character vulnerability moment, shows Larry's deep insecurities

dialogue.line: Fat lips.

jokes.text: I've earned the right to leave the room.
jokes.setup: Larry's endless insecurity spiral about his appearance
jokes.punchline: Paula's fed-up declaration of independence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant line delivery - the formality of 'earned the right' is perfect comedy

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: I've earned the right to leave the room.

jokes.text: Hard-on cream? No, I forgot it.
jokes.setup: Going through Hank's personal supplies list
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of erectile dysfunction cream
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - the casual nature of the request makes it funnier

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Hard-on cream? No, I forgot it.

jokes.text: I could never talk this way around darlene. She'd just get all insulted.
jokes.setup: Hank enjoying talking freely with Brian
jokes.punchline: Revealing his relationship dynamic with Darlene
jokes.explanation: Good character insight into Hank's relationship dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I could never talk this way around darlene. She'd just get all insulted.

jokes.text: I'll be in my office whacking off. Okay. Uh, I'll hold all your calls.
jokes.setup: Hank leaving for his office
jokes.punchline: Brian's professional response to inappropriate information
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - Brian's professional response to TMI is brilliant

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I'll be in my office whacking off.

dialogue.speaker: Brian
dialogue.line: Okay. Uh, I'll hold all your calls.

jokes.text: The Chinese call it a feng shui, the placement of objects in a room to maximize your harmony and power.
jokes.setup: Hank asking about room rearrangement
jokes.punchline: Brian's earnest New Age explanation
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment for Brian's earnest nature

dialogue.speaker: Brian
dialogue.line: The Chinese call it a feng shui, the placement of objects in a room to maximize your harmony and power.

jokes.text: Has she lost any weight? No!
jokes.setup: Reading Darlene's caring letter about Paula
jokes.punchline: Paula's defensive reaction to weight question
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Paula's sensitivity about weight

dialogue.line: Has she lost any weight?

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: No!

jokes.text: You know, the toughest people are the ones who can cry. I'm tough.
jokes.setup: Hank crying and feeling pathetic
jokes.punchline: Brian's earnest reassurance leading to Hank's defensive claim
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing both Brian's earnestness and Hank's fragility

dialogue.speaker: Brian
dialogue.line: You know, the toughest people are the ones who can cry.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I'm tough.

jokes.text: Hey, life, take your best shot, 'cause to Mr. Hank Kingsley, life is a carousel, and the sun is a scoop of ice cream.
jokes.setup: Brian trying to inspire Hank
jokes.punchline: Overwrought motivational speech with absurd metaphors
jokes.explanation: Great character writing - Brian's earnest but absurd motivational style

dialogue.speaker: Brian
dialogue.line: Hey, life, take your best shot, 'cause to Mr. Hank Kingsley, life is a carousel, and the sun is a scoop of ice cream.

jokes.text: Are you gay? Yeah, but that was two years ago, and I got over it.
jokes.setup: Hank processing Brian's sexuality revelation
jokes.punchline: Brian's naive understanding of sexual orientation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant line - perfectly naive and innocent while being completely absurd about sexuality

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Are you gay?

dialogue.speaker: Brian
dialogue.line: Yeah, but that was two years ago, and I got over it.

jokes.text: It's Steve. Steve. God damn it!
jokes.setup: Larry calling someone Sam instead of Steve
jokes.punchline: Larry's frustrated reaction to his own mistake
jokes.explanation: Shows Larry's self-absorption and frustration with his own failings

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: It's Steve. Steve. God damn it!

jokes.text: Well, that's 'cause it's 50 cents now. It just went up a couple years ago. You wouldn't have known that.
jokes.setup: Hank complaining vending machine ate his quarter
jokes.punchline: Larry's condescending explanation about price increase
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry condescension - helpful but patronizing

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, that's 'cause it's 50 cents now. It just went up a couple years ago. You wouldn't have known that.

jokes.text: He's gay? Well, don't tell Dana carvey, 'cause you know what? He'll do an impression of him.
jokes.setup: Hank revealing Brian is gay
jokes.punchline: Larry connecting everything back to his Dana Carvey anxiety
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that shows Larry's complete self-absorption - everything connects to his problems

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: He's gay? Well, don't tell Dana carvey, 'cause you know what? He'll do an impression of him.

jokes.text: A gay? What are you? Italian?
jokes.setup: Hank saying 'if beverly was a gay'
jokes.punchline: Larry mocking Hank's phrasing
jokes.explanation: Good linguistic observation - Larry catching Hank's awkward phrasing

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: A gay? What are you? Italian?

jokes.text: Maybe this will be your first chance to fuck your assistant.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Hank's gay assistant
jokes.punchline: Artie's crude sexual suggestion
jokes.explanation: Typical Artie crudeness. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever or original.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Maybe this will be your first chance to fuck your assistant.

jokes.text: There was not one indication on that resume that he was gay. Not... not... not one.
jokes.setup: Hank wanting to fire Brian
jokes.punchline: Hank's naive expectation that sexuality would be on a resume
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank logic - completely absurd expectation delivered with genuine frustration

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: There was not one indication on that resume that he was gay. Not... not... not one.

jokes.text: Barbara streisand. Aw. Aw, shit.
jokes.setup: Hank complaining about being deceived about Brian's sexuality
jokes.punchline: Artie's dawning realization about the Streisand connection
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to earlier setup - Artie connecting the gay stereotype dots

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Barbara streisand. Aw. Aw, shit.

jokes.text: Yes, I asked for lanolin. I asked for lanolin. Darlene was with me for six years. She never did this. / Hank, you asked for aloe vera.
jokes.setup: Hank berates Brian for getting wrong lotion
jokes.punchline: Brian calmly corrects him - Hank was wrong
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Hank's wrongness exposed while he's being sanctimonious. Character-specific petty tyrant behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Yes, I asked for lanolin. I asked for lanolin. Darlene was with me for six years. She never did this.

dialogue.speaker: Brian
dialogue.line: Hank, you asked for aloe vera.

jokes.text: So this has nothing to do with me being gay, does it?
jokes.setup: Hank firing Brian ostensibly over aloe vera
jokes.punchline: Brian calling out the obvious real reason
jokes.explanation: Good calling out of obvious subtext - Brian showing he's not naive about everything

dialogue.speaker: Brian
dialogue.line: So this has nothing to do with me being gay, does it?

jokes.text: I wore a red ribbon to the emmys. I have been to several elton John concerts.
jokes.setup: Hank defending himself against homophobia charges
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous evidence of gay allyship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hank logic - thinking concert attendance proves allyship

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I wore a red ribbon to the emmys. I have been to several elton John concerts.

jokes.text: I mean, look at my ass. I'm touching my ass.
jokes.setup: Larry obsessing over whether Dana's impression is accurate
jokes.punchline: Larry literally acting out what he denies doing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy - Larry proving the impression right while denying it

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I mean, look at my ass. I'm touching my ass.

jokes.text: You said versace, and you touched yourself. I touched myself on the leg. Why does yourself have to mean penis?
jokes.setup: Dana commenting on Larry's behavior
jokes.punchline: Larry's defensive overexplanation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Larry's paranoia about sexual implications

dialogue.speaker: Dana
dialogue.line: You said versace, and you touched yourself.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I touched myself on the leg.

dialogue.speaker: Dana
dialogue.line: Why does yourself have to mean penis?

jokes.text: People say I flip-flop, and I don't. But then again, I do.
jokes.setup: Dana doing Clinton impression
jokes.punchline: Clinton contradicting himself in the same sentence
jokes.explanation: Good political satire - perfectly captures Clinton's tendency to waffle

dialogue.speaker: Dana
dialogue.line: People say I flip-flop, and I don't. But then again, I do.

jokes.text: Why should I be mad? I have many arrows in my comedic arsenal, Larry. Let's put the Larry Sanders arrow back in the sheath and pull out the Jimmy Stewart arrow. Bing. Bull's-eye!
jokes.setup: Larry accusing Dana of being mad
jokes.punchline: Dana's elaborate archery metaphor for his comedy skills
jokes.explanation: Good extended metaphor that shows Dana's performer mentality

dialogue.speaker: Dana
dialogue.line: Why should I be mad? I have many arrows in my comedic arsenal, Larry. Let's put the Larry Sanders arrow back in the sheath and pull out the Jimmy Stewart arrow. Bing. Bull's-eye!

jokes.text: I'd take that little Dana carvey and I'd dry-shave him with a flicker.
jokes.setup: Discussing Larry's vanity about the impression
jokes.punchline: Artie's bizarre and violent imagery
jokes.explanation: Classic Artie - bizarre, violent, oddly specific imagery that's somehow perfect

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I'd take that little Dana carvey and I'd dry-shave him with a flicker.

jokes.text: I am, but where else am I going to find a personal assistant who'll tell me when Larry is hurt?
jokes.setup: Hank admitting discomfort with Brian being gay
jokes.punchline: Pragmatic reason for keeping him based on gossip value
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Hank's shallow priorities and competitive relationship with Larry.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: I am, but where else am I going to find a personal assistant who'll tell me when Larry is hurt?

jokes.text: Doug llewelyn's a faggot. Sorry.
jokes.setup: Hank's casual homophobia revealed
jokes.punchline: Perfunctory apology showing his ongoing prejudice
jokes.explanation: Character-revealing moment but relies on offensive language. The casual prejudice and weak apology are realistic.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Doug llewelyn's a faggot. Sorry.

jokes.text: I worked on a Cruise ship for eight years. I did nothing but play poker. I am going to kick their asses.
jokes.setup: Hank confident about poker night
jokes.punchline: Brian's hidden expertise revelation
jokes.explanation: Good setup for future episodes and character depth for Brian

dialogue.speaker: Brian
dialogue.line: I worked on a Cruise ship for eight years. I did nothing but play poker. I am going to kick their asses.

jokes.text: Well, I got a boner. Whoa, we're all in trouble.
jokes.setup: Asked if he's feeling lucky for poker
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriate overshare and Artie's mock concern
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry inappropriate comment. Artie's response acknowledges the absurdity without being shocked.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Well, I got a boner.

dialogue.line: Whoa, we're all in trouble.
